snippet.title: GEORGIA’S REP. CLYDE: Pressure Tactics Used On Debt Ceiling To Ensure Repeal Of Pistol Brace Rule
snippet.description:
Dana (@DLoesch / @DanaLoeschRadio) is joined by Georgia’s Rep. Andrew Clyde (GA-9) - @Rep_Clyde on what he was told prior to the passing of the Debt Ceiling Bill.

House leadership told Clyde that he would need to vote FOR the Debt Ceiling Bill if his bill pushing back against that ATF Pistol Brace rule (it’s not LAW) has a chance to ever make it to the floor.

See the story here: https://twitter.com/gunowners/status/1664680646348767241

For those who do not know, the pistol brace was designed for disabled veterans to help them stabilize their hand gun. ATF had NO PROBLEM with the brace until recently and are now trying to make it ‘illegal’.

snippet.title: House Republicans Introduce Bills To Defund Trump Prosecutions Until After Election
snippet.description:
Republicans in the House of Representatives have officially begun their campaign to save Donald Trump, with Representative Andrew Clyde of Georgia introducing two amendments to appropriations bills that would cut the funding for the prosecutions of Trump until after the 2024 election. These Republicans are abusing their power to save the corrupt leader of their Party, and President Biden and the Democrats need to get out in front of this before it is too late. Ring of Fire's Farron Cousins explains what's happening. 

Link - https://thehill.com/homenews/house/4174557-house-republican-aims-to-defund-trump-prosecutions-until-2024-election/

Check out our merch by visiting our store:  https://www.buyrof.com/

Subscribe to our podcast: http://www.ROFPodcast.com

Become a member today!: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCYWIEbibRcZav6xMLo9qWWw/join

Support us by becoming a monthly patron on Patreon, and help keep progressive media alive!: https://www.patreon.com/TheRingofFire

Spread the word! LIKE and SHARE this video or leave a comment to help direct attention to the stories that matter. And SUBSCRIBE to stay connected with Ring of Fire's video content!

Support Ring of Fire by subscribing to our YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/theringoffire

Be sociable! Follow us on:
Facebook: http://www.facebook.com/RingofFireRadio
Twitter: https://twitter.com/RingofFireMedia
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ringoffirenetwork/

*This transcript was generated by a third-party transcription software company, so please excuse any typos.

Republicans are so hellbent on making sure that Donald Trump is legally allowed to do illegal things, that they have now introduced two pieces of legislation in the appropriations committee. One piece of legislation would cut all federal funding for the federal prosecutions of Donald Trump, and the other would cut all federal funding for any state level prosecutions into Donald Trump. Now, these two pieces of legislation have actually been filed as amendments to appropriation bills by appropriations member, uh, Republican representative Andrew Clyde of Georgia. Now we have had other Republicans say that they're gonna defund the investigations. Marjorie Taylor Green has said it, Matt Gaetz has said it, but so far Clyde is the first one to actually do it because of course he's on the appropriations committee. So he gets the first crack at it. And these appropriations bills, by the way, have to be passed by September 30th, or else the government will likely go into shutdown mode. That's why he's put these amendments onto these pieces of legislation 'cause he knows they have to pass. So why not go ahead and just stick it to the federal government by saying, I am taking away all of your money to prosecute Donald Trump. 

This is ridiculous. Here's what it says. Uh, his amendment says it would prohibit the use of federal funding for the prosecution of any major presidential candidate prior to the upcoming presidential election on November 5th, 2024. So it's not, it's it's not about Trump, really, it's about any presidential candidate. 'cause you know, we, in addition to Trump out there being investigated, you, uh, uh hmm, nope, it's pretty much just about Donald Trump, isn't it? But you didn't wanna put that, like, you didn't wanna have that, you didn't wanna make it too obvious. So you're like, if, if there's any presidential candidates, you know, be being investigated, uh, you can't do that. So I have a question for you. You've put forth legislation now at the appropriations committee level that would prohibit funding federal funding from being used to prosecute any presidential candidate before next year's election. Yet at the same time, what is your party in Congress trying to do? You are trying to impeach President Biden, which impeachment is actually a form of prosecution. So if prosecuting a presidential candidate suddenly becomes not something the government can fund, what does that mean for your salary? What does that mean for funding Congress? Right? I mean, you didn't really think this through, did you? Because I love that you tried to make the wording vague enough to where it wasn't just protecting 

Donald Trump, but now you've set your own trap for the Republican party because you're technically kind of making it illegal for them to move forward with their impeachment of Biden. You know, sometimes it's always best to think before you act, but that is not a lesson that Republicans ever want to learn.

localized.description:
This world premiere musical adaptation of the acclaimed novel by F. Scott Fitzgerald tells the impassioned tale of eccentric and mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby in his tragic pursuit of Daisy Buchanan, the woman he’s loved since his youth. Directed by Marc Bruni (Beautiful), this story of extravagance and longing in the lavish Roaring Twenties features a book by Kait Kerrigan (The Mad Ones) and a jazz- and pop-influenced original score by Jason Howland and Nathan Tysen (Paradise Square).
